hypothesis: Enhancing steric complementarity with SSTR2-unique ECL2/ECL3/TM5/TM6 and disrupting off-target electrostatic interactions will increase the selectivity margin (Δmargin > 0). Specifically, introducing aromatic residues at position 5 to stabilize TM5 contacts and charged residues at position 6 to disrupt off-target interactions will improve SSTR2 selectivity.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current candidates are achieving strong SSTR2 binding and selectivity (Δmargin > 0), but structural quality is poor. The next step is to enforce tighter structural constraints and focus mutations at non-pharmacophore positions to improve stability and docking performance.

proposed_changes.rationale: Reducing the allowed clash score will enforce better packing and reduce structural artifacts that lead to poor pLDDT and docking scores.
Focus mutations at non-pharmacophore positions to disrupt conserved-pocket interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5, which are likely causing off-target binding.
